title: feuilleea vulpina
intro: Feuilleea vulpina, also known as bearberry, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to Europe and Asia. It is a member of the heath family and its name is derived from the reddish-purple berries that resemble bear droppings. The leaves and berries of the feuilleea vulpina plant have been used in traditional medicine for their astringent and antiseptic properties, which can help to treat urinary tract infections, diarrhea, and inflammatory conditions. In herbal preparations, the leaves and berries are typically dried and made into tea, capsules, or tinctures, which can be used to support kidney health and prevent the recurrence of UTIs.
